columns.name: VulnerabilityDescription
values: A buffer overflow vulnerability in OpenSSL allows remote code execution via crafted TLS handshake
Remote code execution via JNDI lookup in Apache Log4j logging library
Container escape vulnerability in runc allows attackers to break out of container isolation
Authentication bypass in OpenSSH allows unauthenticated remote code execution on affected Linux systems
HTTP request smuggling in Apache HTTP Server via malformed Transfer-Encoding headers
Unauthorized access vulnerability in Redis allows remote command execution without authentication
HTTP/2 rapid reset attack enables denial of service against web servers
Use-after-free vulnerability in Linux kernel netfilter allows local privilege escalation to root

columns.name: RemediationSteps
values: Upgrade OpenSSL to version 3.2.1 or later. Apply vendor patches if direct upgrade is not possible.
Upgrade Log4j to 2.17.1+. Set log4j2.formatMsgNoLookups=true as interim mitigation.
Update container runtime (runc) to 1.1.12 or later. Restrict container privileges.
Update OpenSSH to 9.8p1 or later. Apply LoginGraceTime restrictions as interim mitigation.
Update Apache HTTP Server to 2.4.60 or later. Review proxy configurations.
Enable Redis AUTH, bind to localhost, and upgrade to 7.2.4 or later.
Apply HTTP/2 patches from your web server vendor. Consider rate limiting as interim mitigation.
Update Linux kernel to the latest patched version for your distribution.
